pi_items.source: Explicitly requested in prompt and checked by export_result.sh.
Computed via PySAM.Pvwattsv8 using the downloaded TMY3 Anchorage.epw and a 250 kW system. Depending on fixed roof vs open rack, the baseline is ~211,721 to 227,581 kWh.
Computed via PySAM.Pvwattsv8 using the downloaded TMY3 Anchorage.epw and a 250 kW system. Depending on fixed roof vs open rack, the baseline is ~211,721 to 227,581 kWh.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real TMY3 EPW weather file for Anchorage downloaded from energyplus-weather.s3.amazonaws.com. A PySAM Pvwattsv8 calculation confirms the baseline energy is ~211,721 to 227,581 kWh, well within the 200,000-270,000 kWh bounds. Notably, the downloaded EPW file has 0.0 for snow depth across the entire year, so enabling the PVWatts snow model will result in exactly 0.0 kWh snow loss. Furthermore, the task is defective because verifier.py is missing entirely from the environment, making the task programmatically unscorable.
